mcqs.question: What is the default access specifier for members of a class in C++?
mcqs.explanation: In C++, class members are private by default unless specified otherwise with access specifiers.

mcqs.question: Which access specifier allows derived classes to access base class members?
mcqs.explanation: The protected access specifier allows members to be accessed within the class and by derived classes.

mcqs.question: What is the main purpose of encapsulation in OOP?

options.text: To hide implementation details and protect data integrity

mcqs.explanation: Encapsulation binds data and functions together while hiding implementation details and protecting data from unauthorized access.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to define member functions outside the class definition?

options.text: None of the above, we use scope resolution operator

mcqs.explanation: The scope resolution operator (::) is used to define member functions outside the class definition, specifying which class they belong to.

mcqs.question: What is the fundamental difference between a struct and a class in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The only fundamental difference is that struct members are public by default, while class members are private by default. All other features are identical.
